diagrams.title: Circular Array Deque Operations
diagrams.description: Shows the four fundamental operations on a circular array deque with front and rear pointers moving logically around the array
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how elements are inserted and deleted at both ends with wrap-around behavior, and understand the relationship between front, rear, and the n-1 maximum elements constraint

diagrams.title: Input-Restricted vs Output-Restricted Deque
diagrams.description: Comparison diagram showing allowed and disallowed operations for each deque type
elements: IRDQ: box with insertRear arrow, deleteFront arrow, deleteRear arrow (insertFront crossed)
ORDQ: box with insertFront arrow, insertRear arrow, deleteFront arrow (deleteRear crossed)
Examples: IRDQ used in task scheduling, ORDQ in palindrome checking
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the difference between input-restricted and output-restricted deques, helping students understand which operations are allowed in each variant and their typical applications

diagrams.title: Deque Simulation of Stack and Queue
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how deque operations map to stack and queue behaviors with operation sequences
elements: Deque as Stack: arrows showing insertFront + deleteFront
Deque as Queue: arrows showing insertRear + deleteFront
Table mapping deque methods to stack/queue equivalents
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate that deque is a generalization of both stack and queue, reinforcing the hierarchical relationship between these fundamental data structures
